title: Research & Patents
content:
# Research & Innovation Radar — Week of June 1, 2026

*Curated for NotVerify / Straker.AI — AI-powered enterprise localization QA: agent orchestration, trust scoring, explainability, compliance.*
*Scan date: 2026-06-03. All citations verified against live arXiv / market-report sources.*

---

## Translation & Language AI

### 1. CompactQE: Interpretable Translation QE via Small Open-Weight LLMs
**Source:** Guttmann, Fraś, Nowakowski, Jassem — arXiv:2605.15763 (May 2026)
**Insight:** Small open-weight LLMs (<30B params) in a single pass simultaneously emit a quality score, MQM-style error spans, suggested corrections, and a full post-edition — beating traditional neural metrics on system-level human correlation while keeping data on-prem.
**NotVerify play:** Directly validates the NotVerify thesis that QA can be *interpretable*, not just a scalar. Ship an on-prem / private-deployment QE tier for regulated enterprise clients (the privacy angle is a moat vs. closed-API competitors). Reuse the "score + error spans + suggested fix" output shape as the native NotVerify verdict object.
**Link:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.15763

### 2. Beyond Scalar Scores: RL for Error-Aware QE of Machine Translation
**Source:** arXiv:2602.08600 (Feb 2026)
**Insight:** Integrating error-aware rewards via reinforcement learning lets LLMs *reason* about translation quality rather than output a bare number, producing explanations tied to specific error types.
**NotVerify play:** Underpins the explainability layer — train a verifier whose reward is alignment with MQM error categories, so every trust score ships with a defensible "why." Strong differentiator for audit-grade QA reports.
**Link:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.08600

### 3. LLMs as Annotators for MT Quality Estimation
**Source:** arXiv:2603.10775 (Mar 2026)
**Insight:** Use expensive frontier LLMs to generate MQM-style annotations that *train* cheaper COMET-style models (the PPbMQM GPT-4o prompt), getting competitive segment-level QE at a fraction of inference cost.
**NotVerify play:** A cost-control / margin moat — distil frontier-quality QA into small in-house scorers so NotVerify's per-segment QA cost stays low at enterprise volume. Frames a "teacher-student QA pipeline" feature.
**Link:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.10775

---

## Agent Systems

### 4. Multi-Agent LLM Orchestration Achieves Deterministic, High-Quality Decision Support
**Source:** Philip Drammeh — arXiv:2511.15755 (Nov 2025, rev. Jan 2026)
**Insight:** Across 348 controlled trials, multi-agent orchestration hit a 100% actionable-recommendation rate vs. 1.7% for single-agent, with *zero quality variance* — enabling production SLAs impossible with single-agent inconsistency. Introduces a Decision Quality (DQ) metric (validity + specificity + correctness).
**NotVerify play:** Hard external evidence for the orchestration architecture — cite the "zero variance enables SLA commitments" finding directly in enterprise sales decks. Adopt a DQ-style composite metric for the NotVerify agent panel (translate / verify / explain agents).
**Link:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2511.15755

### 5. Multi-Agent Collaboration via Evolving Orchestration ("Puppeteer")
**Source:** arXiv:2505.19591 (2025/2026)
**Insight:** A centralized "puppeteer" orchestrator dynamically directs agent "puppets" against evolving task state, promoting effective agents and pruning weak ones — improving both task performance and communication efficiency.
**NotVerify play:** Blueprint for a self-tuning QA conductor that routes each segment to the best-performing verifier agent and retires underperformers. Maps cleanly onto the "Nake the Conductor" orchestration layer; supports a learned-routing feature and efficiency story (fewer tokens per QA pass).
**Link:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2505.19591

---

## Trust & Explainability

### 6. When Can We Trust LLM Graders? Calibrating Confidence for Automated Assessment
**Source:** Ferrer, Turgut, Chen, Sonkar — arXiv:2603.29559 (Mar 2026)
**Insight:** Across seven models, **self-reported confidence consistently gives the best calibration** — beating self-consistency voting — enabling selective automation: confident verdicts auto-process, uncertain ones route to human review.
**NotVerify play:** This *is* the trust-scoring product. Implement a calibrated-confidence gate: high-confidence QA verdicts auto-approve, low-confidence segments escalate to human linguists. Sell the human-in-the-loop economics ("only review what the model is unsure about").
**Link:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.29559

### 7. Know When You're Wrong: Aligning Confidence with Correctness for LLM Error Detection
**Source:** arXiv:2603.06604 (Feb 2026)
**Insight:** Extracts confidence directly from output probabilities and calibrates it to true correctness, sharpening error detection.
**NotVerify play:** Calibration backbone for the trust score — ensures a "92% confident" QA verdict actually means ~92% reliable, which is essential for any compliance-grade or contractual SLA claim. De-risks the trust-score promise legally.
**Link:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.06604

### 8. GraphCompliance: Aligning Policy and Context Graphs for LLM-Based Regulatory Compliance
**Source:** Chung, Ko, Yoo, Onizuka, Kim, Kim, Shin — arXiv:2510.26309 (2025/2026)
**Insight:** Neuro-symbolic framework builds a policy graph and a situational-fact graph, aligns them deterministically, *then* applies LLM reasoning — +4.1–7.2 pts micro-F1 over LLM-only and RAG baselines on 300 GDPR scenarios, with lower false positives.
**NotVerify play:** Direct path to a compliance-QA module — represent client regulatory obligations (EU AI Act high-risk rules apply Aug 2, 2026; GDPR data-provenance) as a policy graph and check localized content against it. Deterministic alignment + LLM reasoning is exactly the audit-defensible structure regulated clients demand.
**Link:** https://arxiv.org/abs/2510.26309

---

## Market Research

### 9. Language Services / Localization Market — 2026 Sizing
**Sources:** Mordor Intelligence; Fortune Business Insights; Localization Software (Global Market Statistics)
**Insight:** Language services market sized at **~USD 75–81B in 2026** (Mordor ~$75.5B; Fortune ~$81.5B → $147B by 2034, 7.6% CAGR). Post-edited MT was **38.9% of 2025 investment**; AI-enabled localization rising ~7.8% CAGR. Localization *software* ~USD 2.33B in 2026 → $4.87B by 2035 (7.5% CAGR). North America = ~37% of sales; APAC growing fastest.
**NotVerify play:** TAM framing for the deck — NotVerify sits in the fast-growing AI-enabled-localization + localization-software slices, not the slow-growth pure-services base. PEMT dominance (39% of spend) is precisely the segment that needs automated QA, so position NotVerify as the QA layer riding the PEMT wave.
**Links:** https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/language-services-market | https://www.fortunebusinessinsights.com/language-services-market-111514

### 10. AI Agents / Agentic AI Market — 2026 Sizing
**Sources:** Grand View Research; Precedence Research; Mordor Intelligence; Gartner (via reports)
**Insight:** AI agents market **~USD 9.9–11.6B in 2026** (up from ~$7.6B in 2025), forecast 40–50% CAGR to $139–295B by 2033–2035. Gartner: by end-2026, **40% of enterprise apps will embed task-specific agents**; ~51% of enterprises already run agents in production; reported avg ROI ~171% (192% in US).
**NotVerify play:** Validates the agentic-orchestration positioning at exactly the adoption inflection. Use the "40% of enterprise apps by end-2026" and "171% ROI" stats in board/investor materials. Reinforces that an agent-native QA platform is timed to the wave, not ahead of it.
**Links:** https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/ai-agents-market-report | https://www.precedenceresearch.com/ai-agents-market

---

## Synthesis — So What for NotVerify

- **Three converging tailwinds validate the whole thesis:** interpretable QE (CompactQE, error-aware RL), production multi-agent orchestration with measurable determinism (Drammeh DQ), and calibrated trust scoring (LLM-grader confidence). The research has caught up to the NotVerify architecture.
- **Strongest near-term moats:** (1) on-prem interpretable QE for privacy-sensitive enterprises, (2) calibrated-confidence human-in-the-loop gating to slash review cost, (3) neuro-symbolic compliance graph keyed to the Aug 2, 2026 EU AI Act high-risk deadline.
- **Sales ammunition:** "zero quality variance enables SLAs" (Drammeh), "self-reported confidence best-calibrated" (Ferrer et al.), and the market stats (40% of enterprise apps agentic by end-2026; ~$80B language-services TAM).
- **Watch item:** No clean, recent *patent* filing on agentic MT-QA orchestration surfaced this week — likely white space. Recommend a targeted patent-landscape pull (USPTO/WIPO PATENTSCOPE) next cycle to confirm IP defensibility before competitors file.
